frontier_justification: Native multimodal models (those trained jointly on vision and language from scratch) represent the current frontier in 2025-2026, with Gemini 2.5, GPT-4o, and InternVL3 competing. The lineage from contrastive pretraining to native generation is actively evolving.

papers.title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
papers.key_contribution: CLIP: contrastive pretraining on 400M image-text pairs enables zero-shot visual classification by aligning visual and language representations.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Supervised vision models require large labeled datasets; do not generalize to new visual concepts.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Contrastive learning: image and text encoders trained to maximize cosine similarity of matched pairs and minimize unmatched pairs via InfoNCE loss.
idea_genome.observation_genome: CLIP matches ResNet-101 on ImageNet zero-shot; strong transfer across 30 datasets; robust to distribution shift.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Encodes image as a single vector; cannot generate language; no spatial understanding; no multi-turn dialogue capability.

papers.title: Flamingo: a Visual Language Model for Few-Shot Learning
papers.key_contribution: Flamingo: bridges frozen vision encoder and frozen LLM via Perceiver Resampler and cross-attention; few-shot visual question answering.

idea_genome.problem_genome: CLIP-type models cannot generate language; VLMs require full fine-tuning, destroying general capabilities.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Freeze both vision encoder (NFNet) and LLM (Chinchilla); insert Perceiver Resampler to compress visual tokens; cross-attention layers in LLM for visual conditioning.
idea_genome.observation_genome: Flamingo achieves state-of-the-art few-shot results on VQA, COCO captioning, and video QA; interleaved text-image inputs.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Heavy architectural complexity; frozen LLM limits joint optimization; one-way: vision→language, no visual generation; expensive inference.

papers.title: Visual Instruction Tuning
papers.key_contribution: LLaVA: simple linear projection connecting CLIP encoder to LLaMA; instruction tuning on GPT-4-generated visual conversations.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Flamingo's Perceiver Resampler adds complexity; need simpler, open-source VLM with strong instruction following.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Linear projection layer maps CLIP visual tokens to LLM token space; fine-tune on GPT-4-generated multimodal instruction data.
idea_genome.observation_genome: LLaVA matches Flamingo on VQA tasks despite 10x fewer parameters; strong conversational VQA; open-source.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Low image resolution (224×224); single image input; linear projection loses spatial structure; limited OCR capability.

papers.title: LLaVA-NeXT: Improved Reasoning, OCR, and World Knowledge
papers.key_contribution: LLaVA-NeXT: dynamic high-resolution (up to 4× tiles), improved data curation (558K LAION + curated instruction data), better reasoning and OCR.

idea_genome.problem_genome: LLaVA 1.5 limited to 336×336 resolution; poor OCR, chart reading, and high-resolution detail understanding.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Dynamic high-resolution: split images into tiles (up to 2×2 + thumbnail); process each tile independently with same CLIP encoder; concatenate tokens.
idea_genome.observation_genome: LLaVA-NeXT outperforms LLaVA-1.5 on OCR (+23%), chart QA (+15%), and document understanding benchmarks.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Token count scales with number of tiles, increasing memory; still two-stage training; no video; limited cross-tile spatial reasoning.

papers.title: InternVL2.5: Expanding Performance Boundaries of Open-Source Multimodal Models with Model, Data, and Test-Time Scaling
papers.key_contribution: InternVL2.5: progressively scaled vision encoder (InternViT-6B) + MLP connector + InternLM2; achieves frontier open-source multimodal performance.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Open-source VLMs lag proprietary models (GPT-4V, Gemini); bottleneck in vision encoder capacity and training data quality.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Scale vision encoder to 6B parameters (InternViT-6B); progressive training with dynamic high-resolution; curated multi-task instruction data at scale.
idea_genome.observation_genome: InternVL2.5-78B achieves top open-source results on MMBench, MathVista, OCRBench, VideoMME; competitive with GPT-4o.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: Still two-stage vision+language; inference overhead from large ViT; not natively trained on interleaved video+text; no audio modality.

papers.title: InternVL-U: Democratizing Unified Multimodal Models for Understanding, Reasoning, Generation and Editing
papers.key_contribution: 4B unified multimodal model supporting understanding, generation, and editing in a single framework; extends InternVL toward native image generation without separate generative model; outperforms unified baselines 3× larger such as BAGEL-14B.

idea_genome.problem_genome: Two-stage VLMs (InternVL2.5) separate understanding and generation; no single open-source model unifies all four tasks (understanding, reasoning, generation, editing) efficiently.
idea_genome.mechanism_genome: Modality-specific design with decoupled visual representations and reasoning-focused data synthesis pipeline; integrates understanding VLM with image generation capability in a unified 4B framework.
idea_genome.observation_genome: InternVL-U 4B consistently outperforms unified baseline models >3× larger; maintains strong comprehension abilities while enabling generation and editing.
idea_genome.limitation_genome: 4B scale trades off against frontier-level performance; unified architecture has not been demonstrated at 70B+ scale where specialized models still dominate on both understanding and generation benchmarks.

edges.evolution_focus: CLIP's visual representations radiated to VLM generation task: frozen NFNet-F6 vision encoder (pretrained with contrastive loss) used as foundation for language-conditional visual reasoning via cross-attention.
Simplifies Flamingo's Perceiver Resampler to a single linear projection; same frozen-encoder + bridge + LLM paradigm, but dramatically simpler and open-source.
Dynamic high-resolution tiling addresses LLaVA's resolution limitation; same architecture (CLIP + projection + LLM), extends input processing.
Combines dynamic resolution (LLaVA-NeXT) with scaled vision encoder (InternViT-6B inspired by earlier work) and progressive multi-task training into a unified open-source frontier model.

InternVL-U hybridizes InternVL2.5's understanding VLM with image generation capability; fuses two previously separate lineages (understanding VLMs and generative image models) into a single unified 4B framework.

open_question: InternVL-U unifies understanding and generation but at 4B scale trades off against frontier-level performance. What architecture and training regime would enable a frontier-scale multimodal model to support understanding, reasoning, generation, and editing without catastrophic cross-task interference, modality imbalance, or safety regressions compared with specialized systems?

question.name: Question
question.description: Present only the domain/subfield and the curated May-2026 research question; ask the model to propose a next-step research direction in Computer Vision / Multimodal Learning.

library.name: Library
library.description: Present the curated research frontier plus 6 trace papers in random order with paper titles and brief abstracts, but without explicit lineage order, genome fields, or dynamics labels; ask the model to propose the next research direction.

lineage.name: Lineage
lineage.description: Present the curated research frontier plus the ordered trace with genome metadata, GenomeDiff-style change evidence, and parent-child relationships; ask the model to predict the next evolutionary step without naming predefined category labels.

frontier_evidence.title: InternVL-U: Democratizing Unified Multimodal Models for Understanding, Reasoning, Generation and Editing
frontier_evidence.unresolved_gap: 4B scale trades off against frontier-level performance; unified architecture has not been demonstrated at 70B+ scale where specialized models still dominate on both understanding and generation benchmarks.
frontier_evidence.why_frontier: Used as the May 2026 frontier anchor: the arena question targets a limitation that remains unresolved at the endpoint of this lineage.

frontier_status.manual_review: Re-audited on 2026-05-05 for appendix release: active arena trace has a 2025/2026 frontier endpoint, a non-solved open research question, valid forward lineage edges, and generation settings aligned with post-hoc dynamics evaluation.
frontier_status.prompt_role: The same research question is shown in Question, Library, and Lineage settings; only the supporting literature/lineage context changes.
